description_long: This object is a medieval-style sword featuring a straight, double-edged blade with a pointed tip, rendered in a metallic gray color. The crossguard is broad and angular, colored in a warm bronze or copper tone, and extends horizontally to provide hand protection. The handle is straight and matches the crossguard in color, topped with a spherical pommel for balance and grip security. The blade appears to be forged from metal, while the hilt components are likely made of metal or wood with a metallic finish. The overall design is simple and functional, with no visible engravings or decorative elements. The proportions suggest it is a one-handed sword, suitable for display or practical use.
description: A medieval-style sword with a straight, double-edged metallic blade, bronze-colored crossguard, and matching handle with a spherical pommel. The design is simple and functional, lacking ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, wide crossguard, and spherical pommel.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the rectangular cross-section of the handle and guard.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and pommel from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and the handle's rectangular profile.
attribute_handle_design: straight, bronze-colored, with spherical pommel, no visible grip texture
